title: update build.py as to use env prioritized
body: Developers would prefer to build the src locally thus taking their own `.env` (if not exists then use env.example) to build the final `CursorPro` executable. Do you like it @chengazhen ?

step_plan.summary: Modify build.py so the build process prefers an existing local .env (copying it into dist/ as .env) and falls back to .env.example. Replace platform-specific shell copy commands with shutil.copyfile and call the centralized filter_output for stderr filtering.

steps.operation: Update and harden stderr filtering helper
steps.reason: Centralize and standardize stderr filtering so build() can call one helper instead of duplicating filtering logic inline. This simplifies maintenance and ensures consistent filtering for subprocess output across the module.
steps.side_effects: Modifying filter_output can affect any callers in the repository that rely on the previous exact output format. Unit tests that assert specific stderr content or formatting must be updated. No external API changes; only internal behavior for logging/diagnostics may change.

steps.operation: Prefer local .env and switch to shutil.copyfile; make build() call filter_output for stderr filtering
steps.reason: Implement requested behavior: when packaging, prefer the developer's local .env (copy it to the dist output as .env). Fall back to .env.example only if .env is absent. Replace platform-specific shell copy commands with shutil.copyfile for cross-platform reliability and avoid shell=True. Call filter_output(result.stderr) instead of duplicating stderr filtering logic within build(). Add import shutil at the top of the file.
steps.side_effects: Changes how the dist/ output is produced: builds run locally will now include developers' .env when present, which affects the packaged application's runtime configuration (good). Tests and scripts that assumed .env.example is always used will need updating. Replacing subprocess copy calls with Python file I/O reduces OS-dependent behavior and avoids shell invocation risks, but might slightly change error messages/tracebacks — update any tests that check exact subprocess stderr output. No external API changes; however the packaged artifact content changes (config behavior at runtime).
